titles.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.en: Chapter 117 – What is Permissible for the owned slaves from the women

titles.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.en: Chapter 117 – What is Permissible for the owned slaves from the women

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْعَبْدِ يَتَزَوَّجُ أَرْبَعَ حَرَائِرَ قَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ يَتَزَوَّجُ حُرَّتَيْنِ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ تَزَوَّجَ أَرْبَعَ إِمَاءٍ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْعَبْدِ يَتَزَوَّجُ أَرْبَعَ حَرَائِرَ قَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ يَتَزَوَّجُ حُرَّتَيْنِ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ تَزَوَّجَ أَرْبَعَ إِمَاءٍ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, and Safwan, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the slave marrying four free women. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but he can marry two free women, and if he so desires to, he can marry four slave girls’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, and Safwan, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the slave marrying four free women. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but he can marry two free women, and if he so desires to, he can marry four slave girls’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ وَ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ وَ صَفْوَانَ عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ رَزِينٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا ( عليهما السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.en: Chapter 117 – What is Permissible for the owned slaves from the women

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ مَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ فَقَالَ حُرَّتَانِ أَوْ أَرْبَعُ إِمَاءٍ قَالَ وَ لَا بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَأْذَنَ لَهُ مَوْلَاهُ فَيَشْتَرِيَ مِنْ مَالِهِ إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ جَارِيَةً أَوْ جَوَارِيَ يَطَؤُهُنَّ وَ رَقِيقُهُ لَهُ حَلَالٌ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ مَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ فَقَالَ حُرَّتَانِ أَوْ أَرْبَعُ إِمَاءٍ قَالَ وَ لَا بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَأْذَنَ لَهُ مَوْلَاهُ فَيَشْتَرِيَ مِنْ مَالِهِ إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ جَارِيَةً أَوْ جَوَارِيَ يَطَؤُهُنَّ وَ رَقِيقُهُ لَهُ حَلَالٌ .

segments: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazal Bin Shazaan, altogether from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Ibn Muskan, from Al Hassan Bin Ziyad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the owned slave, ‘What is Permissible for him from the women?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Two free women, or four slave girls’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem if his master was to permit for him, so he buys from his wealth, if there was a slave girl for him, or slave girls he could go to, and his slave girls would be Permissible for him’.
en.hubeali.text:
Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, and Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazal Bin Shazaan, altogether from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Ibn Muskan, from Al Hassan Bin Ziyad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the owned slave, ‘What is Permissible for him from the women?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Two free women, or four slave girls’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem if his master was to permit for him, so he buys from his wealth, if there was a slave girl for him, or slave girls he could go to, and his slave girls would be Permissible for him’.

narratorChain.raw: أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الْأَشْعَرِيُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ شَاذَانَ جَمِيعاً عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ يَحْيَى عَنِ ابْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.en: Chapter 117 – What is Permissible for the owned slaves from the women

segments: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ كَمْ يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ قَالَ حُرَّتَانِ أَوْ أَرْبَعُ إِمَاءٍ وَ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ إِنْ كَانَ فِي يَدِهِ مَالٌ وَ كَانَ مَأْذُوناً لَهُ فِي التِّجَارَةِ أَنْ يَتَسَرَّى مَا شَاءَ مِنَ الْجَوَارِي وَ يَطَأَهُنَّ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ كَمْ يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ قَالَ حُرَّتَانِ أَوْ أَرْبَعُ إِمَاءٍ وَ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ إِنْ كَانَ فِي يَدِهِ مَالٌ وَ كَانَ مَأْذُوناً لَهُ فِي التِّجَارَةِ أَنْ يَتَسَرَّى مَا شَاءَ مِنَ الْجَوَارِي وَ يَطَأَهُنَّ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, and Muhammad Bin Khalid, altogether, from Al Qasim Bin Urwat, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the owned slaves, ‘How many that he marry?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Two free ones, or four slave girls’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem if there was wealth in his hands, and he was permitted for it regarding the business, if he keeps concubines whatever he so desires to from the slave girls, and go to them’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Husayn Bin Saeed, and Muhammad Bin Khalid, altogether, from Al Qasim Bin Urwat, from Ibn Bukeyr, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5<sup>th</sup> or 6<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>), said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the owned slaves, ‘How many that he marry?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Two free ones, or four slave girls’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem if there was wealth in his hands, and he was permitted for it regarding the business, if he keeps concubines whatever he so desires to from the slave girls, and go to them’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ جَمِيعاً عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا ( عليهما السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.en: Chapter 117 – What is Permissible for the owned slaves from the women

segments: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ يَأْذَنُ لَهُ مَوْلَاهُ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَ مِنْ مَالِهِ الْجَارِيَةَ وَ الثِّنْتَيْنِ وَ الثَّلَاثَ وَ رَقِيقُهُ لَهُ حَلَالٌ قَالَ يَحُدُّ لَهُ حَدّاً لَا يُجَاوِزُهُ .
arabic.text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَمْلُوكِ يَأْذَنُ لَهُ مَوْلَاهُ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَ مِنْ مَالِهِ الْجَارِيَةَ وَ الثِّنْتَيْنِ وَ الثَّلَاثَ وَ رَقِيقُهُ لَهُ حَلَالٌ قَالَ يَحُدُّ لَهُ حَدّاً لَا يُجَاوِزُهُ .

segments: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Is’haq Bin Ammar who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the owned slave whose master had permitted for him that he can buy from his wealth, the slave girl, and two and three, and his enslaved women would be Permissible for him’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He can place limits for him, which he cannot exceed’.
en.hubeali.text:
Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Is’haq Bin Ammar who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the owned slave whose master had permitted for him that he can buy from his wealth, the slave girl, and two and three, and his enslaved women would be Permissible for him’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He can place limits for him, which he cannot exceed’.

narratorChain.raw: حُمَيْدُ بْنُ زِيَادٍ عَنِ ابْنِ سَمَاعَةَ عَنْ غَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب مَا يَحِلُّ لِلْمَمْلُوكِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ
titles.en: Chapter 117 – What is Permissible for the owned slaves from the women

segments: إِذَا أَذِنَ الرَّجُلُ لِعَبْدِهِ أَنْ يَتَسَرَّى مِنْ مَالِهِ فَإِنَّهُ يَشْتَرِي كَمْ شَاءَ بَعْدَ أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدْ أَذِنَ لَهُ .
arabic.text: إِذَا أَذِنَ الرَّجُلُ لِعَبْدِهِ أَنْ يَتَسَرَّى مِنْ مَالِهِ فَإِنَّهُ يَشْتَرِي كَمْ شَاءَ بَعْدَ أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدْ أَذِنَ لَهُ .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Musa Bin Bakr, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said, ‘When the man permits for his slave that he can keep concubines from his wealth, so he can buy as many as he so desires to, after him happened to have been permitted for it’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Musa Bin Bakr, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said, ‘When the man permits for his slave that he can keep concubines from his wealth, so he can buy as many as he so desires to, after him happened to have been permitted for it’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ
